input.prompt:
I'm working in a Go repo at /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-24ebc57a-20260531T183616-5aa0209b/ (the `ddx` CLI, package `cli/internal/agent`). I need to understand the existing implementation around three concerns for the execute-bead loop. Read code thoroughly and report exact file:line references and current behavior.

Concern 1 — "failed attempt leaves canonical repo clean; escalation retry runs from a clean worktree." 154		if mode == "" {
155			if r.Once {
156				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
157			} else {
158				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
159			}
160		}
161		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
162		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
163			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
164		}

166			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
167		}

292	// state before re-evaluating the queue. Infra failures (no_viable_provider,
293	// provider_connectivity) transition the worker to this state instead of parking
294	// individual beads: beads remain immediately reclaimable, and the worker retries
295	// after this window.
296	const PausedInfraInterval = 2 * time.Minute

298	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
299	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
300	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
301	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
302	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
303	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
304	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
305	//
306	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
307	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
308	//
309	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
310	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
311	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
312	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
313	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
314	// directly.
315	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
316		ctx context.Context,
317		projectRoot string,
318		b *bead.Bead,
319		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
320		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
321		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
322		actor, source string,
323		now func() time.Time,
324	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
325		if now == nil {
326			now = time.Now
327		}
328		if submit == nil {
329			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
330		}
331		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
332		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
333		if err != nil {
334			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
335			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
336			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
337			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
338			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
339				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
340			}
341			return &LandResult{
342				Status:      "preserved",
343				PreserveRef: ref,
344				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
345			}, nil, nil
346		}
347		if outcome.Blocked {
348			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
349			if perr != nil {
350				return nil, outcome, perr
351			}
352			if eventStore != nil {
353				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
354			}
355			return land, outcome, nil
356		}
357		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
358			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
359		}
360		var submitted *LandResult
361		cfg, cfgErr := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
362		_, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}, BeadLandingOptions{
363			LandingAdvancer: func(res *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
364				req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
365				if cfgErr == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Git != nil && len(cfg.Git.PostLandCommand) > 0 {
366					req.PostLandCommand = append([]string(nil), cfg.Git.PostLandCommand...)
367				}
368				land, err := submit(req)
369				if err == nil {
370					submitted = land
371				}
372				return land, err
373			},
374		})
375		return submitted, outcome, landErr
376	}

378	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
379	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
380	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
381	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
382	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
383	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
384	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
385		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
386			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
387				return
388			}
389			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
390			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
391		}
392		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
393			if runtime.Log != nil {
394				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
395			}
396			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
397				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
398				"reason":  reason,
399			})
400			_, berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, "operator_required", reason, at)
401			if berr != nil {
402				if runtime.Log != nil {
403					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
404				}
405			}
406		}
407	
408		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
409		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
410		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
411		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
412			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
413				"depth": storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate),
414				"max":   maxDepth,
415			})
416			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
417				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
418				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
419				Body:      string(overflowBody),
420				Actor:     assignee,
421				Source:    "ddx work",
422				CreatedAt: at,
423			})
424			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
425			return
426		}
427	
428		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
429		if err != nil {
430			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
431			return
432		}
433		if decomp == nil {
434			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
435			return
436		}
437		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
438		if lossyOrEmpty {
439			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
440			return
441		}
442	
443		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(ctx, w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
444		if decompErr != nil {
445			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
446			return
447		}
448		if runtime.Log != nil {
449			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
450		}
451		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
452			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
453			"child_ids": childIDs,
454		})
455	}

457	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
458	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
459	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
460	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
461	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
462		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
463		if hook == nil {
464			return report
465		}
466		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
467			return report
468		}
469		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
470		if err != nil {
471			if runtime.Log != nil {
472				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
473			}
474			return report
475		}
476		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
477		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
478		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
479		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
480			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
481		}
482		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
483			return report
484		}
485		if classificationRecognized {
486			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
487		}
488		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
489		return report
490	}

504	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
505	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
506	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
507	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
508		if d <= 0 {
509			return 0
510		}
511		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
512			return MaxLoopCooldown
513		}
514		return d
515	}

250	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
251	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
252	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
253	// worktree.

255		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
256		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)

263		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a

265		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
266		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)

268		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
269		// other work that might prune it.

272		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.

289	func applyWorkerCandidateCycle(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, wtPath string, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, res *ExecuteBeadResult) error {
290		if res == nil || wtPath == "" || res.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
291			return nil
292		}
293	
294		_ = MarkWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath)
295		defer func() {
296			_ = ClearWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath)
297		}()
298	
299		coord := &AttemptCycleCoordinator{
300			Pass: staticCandidateResultPass{
301				candidate: CandidateResult{
302					Report:       ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, ""),
303					WorktreePath: wtPath,
304					CycleIndex:   res.CycleIndex,
305				},
306			},
307			RefStore:    &GitCandidateRefStore{},
308			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
309			BeadEvents:  runtime.BeadEvents,
310		}
311		cycleResult, err := coord.Run(ctx, res.BeadID)
312		if err != nil {
313			return err
314		}
315		res.CandidateRef = cycleResult.Report.CandidateRef
316		res.CycleIndex = cycleResult.Report.CycleIndex
317		res.CycleTrace = append([]ExecutionCycleTrace(nil), cycleResult.Report.CycleTrace...)
318		return nil
319	}

485	
486	func dirtyWorktreePaths(dir string) []string {
487		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--untracked-files=all").Output()
488		if err != nil {
489			return nil
490		}
491		var paths []string
492		seen := map[string]bool{}
493		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
494			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
495			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" || len(line) < 4 {
496				continue
497			}
498			path := strings.TrimSpace(line[3:])
499			if idx := strings.Index(path, " -> "); idx >= 0 {
500				path = strings.TrimSpace(path[idx+4:])
501			}
502			if path == "" || seen[path] {
503				continue
504			}
505			seen[path] = true
506			paths = append(paths, path)
507		}
508		return paths
509	}

511	// EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
512	// applied when landEvidence / VerifyCleanWorktree stage the evidence
513	// directory. Only excludes the multi-thousand-line embedded session log
514	// (the single file that actually explodes past provider context
515	// limits). prompt.md, usage.json, manifest.json, and result.json remain
516	// tracked — they are small and serve the audit-trail contract. The
517	// gitignore written by `ddx init` already treats .ddx/executions/*/
518	// embedded as ignored; this pathspec is defence-in-depth for force-add
519	// paths.
520	//
521	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896 — session logs landed as tracked
522	// evidence caused retry prompts to balloon past 2M+ tokens and crash
523	// every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
524	func EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs() []string {
525		return []string{
526			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
527		}
528	}
529	
530	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
531	// applied when review-prompt synthesis runs `git show <rev>` over the
532	// evidence commit. Broader than EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs: excludes
533	// prompt.md and usage.json too, because even though they're tracked for
534	// audit, they're execution-artifact noise from the reviewer's
535	// perspective — the reviewer wants to see the implementation diff, not
536	// the prior attempt's prompt or token counters. This also protects
537	// against old commits (pre-fix) that committed the session log
538	// directly.
539	//
540	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896.
541	func EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs() []string {
542		return []string{
543			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
544			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
545			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
546		}
547	}
548	
549	// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise
550	// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)
551	// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit
552	// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.
553	func (r *RealGitOps) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {
554		// Do NOT list already-gitignored paths (.ddx/agent-logs, .ddx/workers) as
555		// :(exclude) pathspecs. Git treats a path named by :(exclude) as explicitly
556		// referenced, so when the path is also .gitignored git emits "The following
557		// paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files" AND exits 1 — even
558		// though the pathspec is trying to SKIP it. Paths already in .gitignore are
559		// excluded by default; excludes here are only for paths that would
560		// otherwise be tracked.
561		addArgs := []string{
562			"add", "-A", "--",
563			".",
564		}
565		addArgs = append(addArgs, synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir)...)
566		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, addArgs...).Run(); err != nil {
567			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging changes: %w", err)
568		}
569		statusOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
570		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(statusOut)) == 0 {
571			return false, nil
572		}
573		if msg == "" {
574			msg = "chore: execute-bead synthesized result commit"
575		}
576		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "commit", "-m", msg).CombinedOutput()
577		if err != nil {
578			return false, fmt.Errorf("synthesize commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
579		}
580		return true, nil
581	}

583	// checkpointPreDispatchDirt captures DDx bookkeeping changes as a commit on
584	// the current branch without using the caller checkout's real index or commit
585	// hooks. The checkpoint is intentionally narrow: if the parent checkout also
586	// contains ordinary implementation files, the attempt fails with an actionable
587	// error so those changes can be committed in the bead's substantive
588	// [ddx-<id>] commit instead of being folded into the checkpoint.
589	const preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix = "checkpoint refused to absorb implementation changes outside DDx bookkeeping: "
590	const preDispatchDirtyPreserveRefPrefix = "refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/"
591	
592	// PreDispatchDirtyPreservation records the recoverable handle DDx created when
593	// it preserved implementation dirt before a watch-mode redispatch.
594	type PreDispatchDirtyPreservation struct {
595		DirtyPaths     []string
596		PreserveRef    string
597		RecoverCommand string
598	}
599	
600	var preDispatchDirtyPathLister = func(projectRoot string) ([]string, error) {
601		return preDispatchCheckpointDirtyPaths(projectRoot)
602	}
603	
604	func normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(paths []string) []string {
605		seen := make(map[string]bool, len(paths))
606		normalized := make([]string, 0, len(paths))
607		for _, path := range paths {
608			path = filepath.ToSlash(strings.TrimSpace(path))
609			if path == "" || seen[path] {
610				continue
611			}
612			seen[path] = true
613			normalized = append(normalized, path)
614		}
615		sort.Strings(normalized)
616		return normalized
617	}
618	
619	func intersectPreDispatchDirtyPaths(current, want []string) []string {
620		if len(current) == 0 || len(want) == 0 {
621			return nil
622		}
623		wantSet := make(map[string]bool, len(want))
624		for _, path := range want {
625			wantSet[path] = true
626		}
627		var overlap []string
628		for _, path := range current {
629			if wantSet[path] {
630				overlap = append(overlap, path)
631			}
632		}
633		sort.Strings(overlap)
634		return overlap
635	}
636	
637	func classifyPreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths []string) (allowedPaths, blockedPaths []string) {
638		for _, path := range normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths) {
639			if preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path) {
640				allowedPaths = append(allowedPaths, path)
641				continue
642			}
643			blockedPaths = append(blockedPaths, path)
644		}
645		return allowedPaths, blockedPaths
646	}
647	
648	// stablePreDispatchImplementationDirtyPaths re-reads the project dirt and only
649	// treats implementation paths as actionable when they survive the immediate
650	// recheck. This filters transient rename/delete paths that can briefly appear
651	// in watch mode right after a successful land.
652	func stablePreDispatchImplementationDirtyPaths(projectRoot string, dirtyPaths []string) ([]string, []string, error) {
653		normalized := normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths)
654		if projectRoot == "" || len(normalized) == 0 {
655			return normalized, normalized, nil
656		}
657	
658		currentPaths, err := preDispatchDirtyPathLister(projectRoot)
659		if err != nil {
660			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("rechecking pre-dispatch dirt: %w", err)
661		}
662		return intersectPreDispatchDirtyPaths(currentPaths, normalized), currentPaths, nil
663	}
664	
665	func resolveOptionalGitRef(dir, ref string) (string, bool) {
666		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "-q", "--verify", ref).Output()
667		if err != nil {
668			return "", false
669		}
670		sha := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
671		if sha == "" {
672			return "", false
673		}
674		return sha, true
675	}
676	
677	func preDispatchDirtyRecoverCommand(preserveRef string) string {
678		if preserveRef == "" {
679			return ""
680		}
681		return "git stash apply " + preserveRef
682	}
683	
684	func preservePreDispatchDirtyPaths(projectRoot string, dirtyPaths []string) (*PreDispatchDirtyPreservation, error) {
685		if projectRoot == "" {
686			return nil, fmt.Errorf("project root is required for pre-dispatch dirty preservation")
687		}
688		normalized := normalizePreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths)
689		if len(normalized) == 0 {
690			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no dirty implementation paths to preserve")
691		}
692	
693		var preserved *PreDispatchDirtyPreservation
694		if err := withTrackerLock(projectRoot, func() error {
695			var err error
696			preserved, err = preservePreDispatchDirtyPathsLocked(projectRoot, normalized)
697			return err
698		}); err != nil {
699			return nil, err
700		}
701		return preserved, nil
702	}
703	
704	func preservePreDispatchDirtyPathsLocked(projectRoot string, dirtyPaths []string) (*PreDispatchDirtyPreservation, error) {
705		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").Run(); err != nil {
706			return nil, fmt.Errorf("verifying project worktree for pre-dispatch preservation: %w", err)
707		}
708		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", "HEAD").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
709			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving HEAD for pre-dispatch preservation: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
710		}
711	
712		stashBefore, _ := resolveOptionalGitRef(projectRoot, "refs/stash")
713		preserveID := GenerateAttemptID()
714		preserveRef := preDispatchDirtyPreserveRefPrefix + preserveID
715		stashMsg := "ddx pre-dispatch preserve " + preserveID
716		stashArgs := []string{"stash", "push", "--include-untracked", "--message", stashMsg, "--"}
717		stashArgs = append(stashArgs, dirtyPaths...)
718		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, stashArgs...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
719			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving pre-dispatch dirty implementation paths: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
720		}
721	
722		stashAfter, ok := resolveOptionalGitRef(projectRoot, "refs/stash")
723		if !ok || stashAfter == "" || stashAfter == stashBefore {
724			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving pre-dispatch dirty implementation paths did not create a recoverable stash entry")
725		}
726		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "update-ref", preserveRef, stashAfter).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
727			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pinning pre-dispatch dirty preservation ref: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
728		}
729		_, _ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "stash", "drop", "stash@{0}").CombinedOutput()
730	
731		remainingPaths, err := preDispatchDirtyPathLister(projectRoot)
732		if err != nil {
733			return nil, fmt.Errorf("verifying preserved pre-dispatch dirt: %w", err)
734		}
735		if overlap := intersectPreDispatchDirtyPaths(remainingPaths, dirtyPaths); len(overlap) > 0 {
736			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving pre-dispatch dirty implementation paths left the project worktree dirty: %s", strings.Join(overlap, ", "))
737		}
738	
739		return &PreDispatchDirtyPreservation{
740			DirtyPaths:     append([]string(nil), dirtyPaths...),
741			PreserveRef:    preserveRef,
742			RecoverCommand: preDispatchDirtyRecoverCommand(preserveRef),
743		}, nil
744	}
745	
746	func checkpointPreDispatchDirt(projectRoot, attemptID string) (bool, error) {
747		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").Run(); err != nil {
748			return false, nil
749		}
750		headOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", "HEAD").Output()
751		if err != nil {
752			return false, nil
753		}
754		head := strings.TrimSpace(string(headOut))
755		if head == "" {
756			return false, nil
757		}
758	
759		skipWorktreePaths, err := checkpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot)
760		if err != nil {
761			return false, err
762		}
763	
764		dirtyPaths, err := preDispatchDirtyPathLister(projectRoot)
765		if err != nil {
766			return false, err
767		}
768		if len(dirtyPaths) == 0 {
769			return false, nil
770		}
771	
772		allowedPaths, blockedPaths := classifyPreDispatchDirtyPaths(dirtyPaths)
773		if len(blockedPaths) > 0 {
774			stableBlockedPaths, currentDirtyPaths, err := stablePreDispatchImplementationDirtyPaths(projectRoot, blockedPaths)
775			if err != nil {
776				return false, err
777			}
778			if len(stableBlockedPaths) > 0 {
779				return false, fmt.Errorf(
780					"%s%s; commit or clean those files before rerunning so the bead's [ddx-<id>] substantive commit stays intentional",
781					preDispatchCheckpointDirtyRefusalPrefix,
782					strings.Join(stableBlockedPaths, ", "),
783				)
784			}
785			allowedPaths, _ = classifyPreDispatchDirtyPaths(currentDirtyPaths)
786		}
787		if len(allowedPaths) == 0 {
788			return false, nil
789		}
790	
791		indexFile, err := config.CreateExecutionScratch(projectRoot, "ddx-pre-dispatch-index-*")
792		if err != nil {
793			return false, fmt.Errorf("creating temp checkpoint index: %w", err)
794		}
795		indexPath := indexFile.Name()
796		_ = indexFile.Close()
797		_ = os.Remove(indexPath)
798		defer func() { _ = os.Remove(indexPath) }()
799	
800		gitWithIndex := func(args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
801			cmd := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, args...)
802			cmd.Env = append(cmd.Env, "GIT_INDEX_FILE="+indexPath)
803			return cmd.CombinedOutput()
804		}
805	
806		if out, err := gitWithIndex("read-tree", "HEAD"); err != nil {
807			return false, fmt.Errorf("initializing checkpoint index: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
808		}
809	
810		addArgs := []string{"add", "-A", "--force", "--"}
811		addArgs = append(addArgs, allowedPaths...)
812		if out, err := gitWithIndex(addArgs...); err != nil {
813			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging checkpoint changes: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
814		}
815	
816		changedOut, err := gitWithIndex("diff", "--cached", "--name-only")
817		if err != nil {
818			return false, fmt.Errorf("checking checkpoint diff: %w", err)
819		}
820		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(changedOut)) == 0 {
821			return false, nil
822		}
823	
824		treeOut, err := gitWithIndex("write-tree")
825		if err != nil {
826			return false, fmt.Errorf("writing checkpoint tree: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(treeOut)), err)
827		}
828		tree := strings.TrimSpace(string(treeOut))
829		msg := "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead " + attemptID
830		commitOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
831			"-c", "user.name=ddx-checkpoint",
832			"-c", "user.email=checkpoint@ddx.local",
833			"commit-tree", tree, "-p", head, "-m", msg,
834		).CombinedOutput()
835		if err != nil {
836			return false, fmt.Errorf("creating checkpoint commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
837		}
838		commit := strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut))
839	
840		refOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "symbolic-ref", "-q", "HEAD").Output()
841		ref := strings.TrimSpace(string(refOut))
842		if ref == "" {
843			ref = "HEAD"
844		}
845		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "update-ref", ref, commit, head).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
846			return false, fmt.Errorf("advancing checkpoint ref: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
847		}
848		if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
849			return false, fmt.Errorf("syncing checkpoint index: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
850		}
851		if err := restoreCheckpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot, skipWorktreePaths); err != nil {
852			return false, err
853		}
854		return true, nil
855	}
856	
857	func checkpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot string) ([]string, error) {
858		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "ls-files", "-t", "-z").Output()
859		if err != nil {
860			return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing skip-worktree paths: %w", err)
861		}
862		var paths []string
863		for len(out) > 0 {
864			recordEnd := bytes.IndexByte(out, 0)
865			if recordEnd == -1 {
866				recordEnd = len(out)
867			}
868			record := out[:recordEnd]
869			if recordEnd < len(out) {
870				out = out[recordEnd+1:]
871			} else {
872				out = nil
873			}
874			if len(record) < 3 || record[0] != 'S' || record[1] != ' ' {
875				continue
876			}
877			path := filepath.ToSlash(string(record[2:]))
878			if path != "" {
879				paths = append(paths, path)
880			}
881		}
882		return paths, nil
883	}
884	
885	func restoreCheckpointSkipWorktreePaths(projectRoot string, paths []string) error {
886		for start := 0; start < len(paths); start += 100 {
887			end := start + 100
888			if end > len(paths) {
889				end = len(paths)
890			}
891			args := append([]string{"update-index", "--skip-worktree", "--"}, paths[start:end]...)
892			if out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, args...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
893				return fmt.Errorf("restoring skip-worktree paths after checkpoint index sync: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
894			}
895		}
896		return nil
897	}
898	
899	// isMaterializedSkillSymlink reports whether a path is a skill directory symlink
900	// that has been materialized into a real directory by an external tool. Skill
901	// symlinks (.crush/skills/*, .claude/skills/*, .agents/skills/*) tracked in git
902	// as symlinks (mode 120000) but present in the working tree as directories are
903	// benign and do not indicate uncommitted implementation changes.
904	func isMaterializedSkillSymlink(projectRoot, path string) bool {
905		// Check if path is in a skill directory root
906		skillDirs := []string{".crush/skills", ".claude/skills", ".agents/skills"}
907		var isSkillPath bool
908		for _, skillDir := range skillDirs {
909			if path == skillDir || strings.HasPrefix(path, skillDir+"/") {
910				isSkillPath = true
911				break
912			}
913		}
914		if !isSkillPath {
915			return false
916		}
917	
918		// Check if it's tracked in git as a symlink
919		mode, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
920			"ls-files", "--stage", "--", path).Output()
921		if err != nil || len(mode) == 0 {
922			return false
923		}
924		// ls-files --stage output is: "<mode> <object> <stage>\t<file>"
925		// We need to extract the mode field (first field, 120000 for symlink)
926		parts := strings.Fields(string(mode))
927		if len(parts) < 1 {
928			return false
929		}
930		gitMode := parts[0]
931		if gitMode != "120000" {
932			return false
933		}
934	
935		// Check if the working tree has it as a directory
936		fullPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(path))
937		info, err := os.Stat(fullPath)
938		if err != nil {
939			return false
940		}
941		return info.IsDir()
942	}
943	
944	func preDispatchCheckpointDirtyPaths(projectRoot string) ([]string, error) {
945		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
946			"status", "--porcelain=v1", "-z", "--untracked-files=all", "--ignored=matching", "--", ".").Output()
947		if err != nil {
948			return nil, fmt.Errorf("listing checkpoint dirt: %w", err)
949		}
950		if len(out) == 0 {
951			return nil, nil
952		}
953	
954		seen := make(map[string]bool)
955		paths := make([]string, 0, 8)
956		for len(out) > 0 {
957			recordEnd := bytes.IndexByte(out, 0)
958			if recordEnd == -1 {
959				recordEnd = len(out)
960			}
961			record := out[:recordEnd]
962			if recordEnd < len(out) {
963				out = out[recordEnd+1:]
964			} else {
965				out = nil
966			}
967			if len(record) < 3 {
968				continue
969			}
970			status := string(record[:2])
971			path := filepath.ToSlash(string(record[3:]))
972			if status == "!!" && !preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path) {
973				continue
974			}
975			if preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath(path) {
976				continue
977			}
978			if isMaterializedSkillSymlink(projectRoot, path) {
979				continue
980			}
981			if path != "" && !seen[path] {
982				seen[path] = true
983				paths = append(paths, path)
984			}
985			if record[0] == 'R' || record[0] == 'C' {
986				recordEnd = bytes.IndexByte(out, 0)
987				if recordEnd == -1 {
988					recordEnd = len(out)
989				}
990				record = out[:recordEnd]
991				if recordEnd < len(out) {
992					out = out[recordEnd+1:]
993				} else {
994					out = nil
995				}
996				path := filepath.ToSlash(string(record))
997				if status == "!!" && !preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path) {
998					continue
999				}
1000				if preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath(path) {
1001					continue
1002				}
1003				if isMaterializedSkillSymlink(projectRoot, path) {
1004					continue
1005				}
1006				if path != "" && !seen[path] {
1007					seen[path] = true
1008					paths = append(paths, path)
1009				}
1010			}
1011		}
1012		return paths, nil
1013	}

1015	func preDispatchCheckpointAllowedPath(path string) bool {
1016		switch {
1017		case path == ".ddx/beads.jsonl":
1018			return true
1019		case path == ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl":
1020			return true
1021		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/executions/"):
1022			return true
1023		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/metrics/"):
1024			return true
1025		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/runs/"):
1026			return true
1027		case path == ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName:
1028			return true
1029		default:
1030			return false
1031		}
1032	}
1033	
1034	func preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredPath(path string) bool {
1035		switch {
1036		case path == ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock":
1037			return true
1038		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/"):
1039			return true
1040		case path == ".ddx/beads.lock":
1041			return true
1042		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/beads.lock/"):
1043			return true
1044		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/attachments/"):
1045			return true
1046		case path == ".ddx/run-state.json":
1047			return true
1048		case path == ".ddx/run-state":
1049			return true
1050		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/run-state/"):
1051			return true
1052		case preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredExecutionEmbeddedPath(path):
1053			return true
1054		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/beads.jsonl.tmp-"):
1055			return true
1056		case path == ".ddx/workers":
1057			return true
1058		case strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/workers/"):
1059			return true
1060		default:
1061			return false
1062		}
1063	}
1064	
1065	func preDispatchCheckpointIgnoredExecutionEmbeddedPath(path string) bool {
1066		const prefix = ".ddx/executions/"
1067		if !strings.HasPrefix(path, prefix) {
1068			return false
1069		}
1070		rest := strings.TrimPrefix(path, prefix)
1071		attemptID, remainder, ok := strings.Cut(rest, "/")
1072		if !ok || attemptID == "" {
1073			return false
1074		}
1075		return remainder == "embedded" || strings.HasPrefix(remainder, "embedded/")
1076	}
1077	
1078	func synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir string) []string {
1079		candidates := []struct {
1080			pathspec    string
1081			ignoreProbe string
1082		}{
1083			{
1084				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
1085				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/embedded",
1086			},
1087			{
1088				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt",
1089				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/no_changes_rationale.txt",
1090			},
1091			// Exclude DDx-managed evidence bundle files written to the attempt
1092			// worktree by execute_bead.go itself. These are committed separately by
1093			// commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree and must not appear in the agent's code
1094			// commit (which SynthesizeCommit creates). Prior to this worktree-evidence
1095			// design, these files lived in projectRoot and were invisible to
1096			// SynthesizeCommit (which runs in wtPath).
1097			{
1098				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
1099				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/prompt.md",
1100			},
1101			{
1102				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/manifest.json",
1103				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/manifest.json",
1104			},
1105			{
1106				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/result.json",
1107				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/result.json",
1108			},
1109			{
1110				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
1111				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/usage.json",
1112			},
1113			{
1114				pathspec:    ":(exclude).claude/skills",
1115				ignoreProbe: ".claude/skills",
1116			},
1117			{
1118				pathspec:    ":(exclude).agents/skills",
1119				ignoreProbe: ".agents/skills",
1120			},
1121			{
1122				pathspec:    ":(exclude)" + ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName,
1123				ignoreProbe: ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName,
1124			},
1125			{
1126				// Tracker-lock coordination dir (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock/{pid,
1127				// acquired_at}). Present while withTrackerLock is held — must
1128				// not be staged by a SynthesizeCommit running inside the lock
1129				// (regression: HEAD-race fix folded SynthesizeCommit into the
1130				// locked critical section, exposing this directory to
1131				// `git add -A`).
1132				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/.git-tracker.lock",
1133				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/pid",
1134			},
1135		}
1136	
1137		pathspecs := make([]string, 0, len(candidates))
1138		for _, c := range candidates {

1281	func startRunStateRefresh(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, state RunState) func() {
1282		interval := RunStateRefreshInterval
1283		if interval <= 0 {
1284			interval = 10 * time.Second
1285		}
1286		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
1287		done := make(chan struct{})
1288		go func() {
1289			defer close(done)
1290			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
1291			defer ticker.Stop()
1292			for {
1293				select {
1294				case <-ctx.Done():
1295					return
1296				case <-ticker.C:
1297					_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, mergeLatestCandidateCycleRunState(projectRoot, state))
1298				}
1299			}
1300		}()
1301		return func() {
1302			cancel()
1303			<-done
1304		}
1305	}

245	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
246	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
247	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
248	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
249	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
250	// bead merged cleanly.
251	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
252		switch landingOutcome {
253		case "merged":
254			return ""
255		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
256			return FailureModeNoChanges
257		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
258			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
259		case "error":
260			if workerMode != "" {
261				return workerMode
262			}
263			return FailureModeUnknown
264		case "preserved":
265			switch {
266			case landingReason == "merge conflict" || landingReason == "merge failed" || landingReason == "ff-merge not possible":
267				return FailureModeMergeConflict
268			case landingReason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(landingReason, PreMergeChecksReason):
269				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
270			case landingReason == RatchetPreserveReason:
271				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
272			case landingReason == AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason:
273				return FailureModeAttemptIntegrity
274			case landingReason == OperatorCancelReason:
275				return ""
276			}
277			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
278			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
279			if workerMode != "" {
280				return workerMode
281			}
282			return FailureModeUnknown
283		}
284		if workerMode != "" {
285			return workerMode
286		}
287		return FailureModeUnknown
288	}

290	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
291	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
292	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
293	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
294	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
295	// test-oriented by convention.
296	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
297		for _, gr := range gateResults {
298			if gr.Status != "fail" {
299				continue
300			}
301			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
302			if containsAny(combined,
303				"build failed",
304				"compilation failed",
305				"compile error",
306				"compilation error",
307				"cannot find package",
308				"undefined:",
309				"undefined reference",
310				"syntax error") {
311				return FailureModeBuildFailure
312			}
313		}
314		return FailureModeTestFailure

406	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
407	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
408	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
409	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
410		if exitCode != 0 {
411			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
412		}
413	
414		switch outcome {
415		case "merged":
416			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
417			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
418			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
419			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
420			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
421			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
422				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
423			}
424			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
425				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
426			}
427			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
428		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
429			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
430		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
431			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
432		case "error":
433			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
434		case "preserved":
435			switch {
436			case reason == "merge conflict" || reason == "merge failed" || reason == "ff-merge not possible":
437				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
438			case reason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(reason, PreMergeChecksReason):
439				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
440			case reason == RatchetPreserveReason:
441				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
442			case reason == AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason:
443				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
444			case reason == OperatorCancelReason:
445				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
446			}
447			if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
448				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
449			}
450			// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
451			// explicitly requested no merge.
452			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
453		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
454			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
455		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
456			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
457		default:
458			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
459		}
460	}

51	// TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue covers watch mode: the loop must
52	// NOT exit when nextCandidate returns no
53	// eligible bead. It must reset the per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps and
54	// sleep, then poll again. Cancelling the context is the only way out.
55	func TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {
56		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
57		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
58	
59		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
60			Store: store,
61			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
62				t.Fatalf("executor must not run on an empty queue")
63				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
64			}),
65		}
66	
67		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
68		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
69	
70		// Use a tiny poll interval so the test runs quickly; cancel after enough
71		// wall-clock for several empty-poll cycles to confirm the loop is still
72		// running and not bailing out after the first empty poll.
73		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
74		pollInterval := 5 * time.Millisecond
75		cancelAfter := 50 * time.Millisecond
76		go func() {
77			time.Sleep(cancelAfter)
78			cancel()
79		}()
80	
81		start := time.Now()
82		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
83			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
84			IdleInterval: pollInterval,
85		})
86		elapsed := time.Since(start)
87	
88		// Context cancellation surfaces as the returned error. The key
89		// invariant: the loop survived past the first empty poll.
90		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
91		require.NotNil(t, result)
92		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
93		assert.True(t, elapsed >= cancelAfter,
94			"loop must run until ctx cancellation; ran for %s, expected >= %s", elapsed, cancelAfter)
95	}

311	// TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail covers
312	// ddx-df77e668 AC #3: when the worker idles on the same pre-claim blocker for
313	// preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold consecutive cycles, it emits a non-terminal
314	// loop.operator_attention event carrying the bead-id, detail, and elapsed-idle
315	// instead of looping silently. The beads are never cooldowned, and the loop
316	// keeps idling (the escalation does not stop the worker).
317	func TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail(t *testing.T) {
318		store, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
319		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
320			Store: store,
321			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
322				t.Errorf("executor must not run while pre-claim idles")
323				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
324			}),
325		}
326	
327		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
328		defer cancel()
329		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 256)
330		done := make(chan struct{})
331		var idleCount int32
332		go func() {
333			for {
334				select {
335				case evt := <-progressCh:
336					if evt.Phase == "loop.idle" && evt.Message == preClaimIdleReasonTrackerContention {
337						if atomic.AddInt32(&idleCount, 1) >= int32(preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)+2 {
338							cancel()
339							return
340						}
341					}
342				case <-done:
343					return
344				}
345			}
346		}()
347		defer close(done)
348	
349		var eventSink, logBuf bytes.Buffer
350		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
351		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
352		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
353			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
354			IdleInterval: time.Millisecond,
355			Log:          &logBuf,
356			EventSink:    &eventSink,
357			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
358			SessionID:    "sess-escalate",
359			WorkerID:     "worker-escalate",
360			PreClaimHook: func(ctx context.Context) error {
361				return errors.New("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s:\nM\t.ddx/beads.jsonl\nM\t.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl")
362			},
363		})
364	
365		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
366		require.NotNil(t, result)
367		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
368		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
369		// The escalation is non-terminal: it does not populate a terminal stop.
370		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
371	
372		// Beads stay open and uncooldowned — tracker contention never faults a bead.
373		gotFirst, err := store.Get(first.ID)
374		require.NoError(t, err)
375		gotSecond, err := store.Get(second.ID)
376		require.NoError(t, err)
377		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotFirst.Status)
378		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotSecond.Status)
379		assert.Nil(t, gotFirst.Extra["work-retry-after"])
380	
381		// Exactly one operator-attention escalation event is emitted for the streak.
382		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
383		var escalations []map[string]any
384		for _, line := range lines {
385			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
386				continue
387			}
388			var entry map[string]any
389			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
390			if entry["type"] != "loop.operator_attention" {
391				continue
392			}
393			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
394			require.True(t, ok)
395			if data["reason"] == "preclaim_idle_escalation" {
396				escalations = append(escalations, data)
397			}
398		}
399		require.Len(t, escalations, 1, "exactly one escalation event per same-detail streak")
400		esc := escalations[0]
401		assert.Contains(t, []any{first.ID, second.ID}, esc["bead_id"], "escalation must name a skipped bead")
402		assert.Contains(t, esc["detail"], ".ddx/beads.jsonl", "escalation must carry the blocker detail")
403		elapsed, ok := esc["elapsed_idle"].(string)
404		require.True(t, ok, "escalation must carry elapsed_idle")
405		assert.NotEmpty(t, elapsed)
406		idleCountVal, ok := esc["idle_count"].(float64)
407		require.True(t, ok)
408		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, int(idleCountVal), preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)
409	
410		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), "operator attention: pre-claim idled")
411	}

   - Canonical repo only receives checkpoint commit (one commit per attempt)

The checkpoint commit stays, and the next attempt runs from the current HEAD (which includes the checkpoint).
